task.title: Even number of a symbols
task.specification:
Design a deterministic finite automaton that recognizes the language of words over the alphabet $\{a,b\}$ in which the number of symbols $a$ is even.
$$L = \{ w \in \{a,b\}^* \mid \#_a(w) = 2n,\ n \in \mathbb{N}_0 \}$$
This means that the word may contain any number of symbols $b$, but the number of symbols $a$ must be divisible by 2.

task.title: Odd length words over {a,b}
task.specification:
Design a deterministic finite automaton that recognizes all words of odd length over the alphabet $\{a,b\}$.
$$L = \{ w \in \{a,b\}^* \mid \#_a(w) + \#_b(w) = 2n + 1,\ n \in \mathbb{N}_0 \}$$
This means that the automaton accepts exactly those words that contain an odd number of symbols.

task.title: Difference between the number of a and b divisible by four
task.specification:
Design a deterministic finite automaton that recognizes the language of words over the alphabet $\{a,b\}$ in which the difference between the number of symbols $a$ and $b$ is divisible by four.
$$L = \{ w \in \{a,b\}^* \mid \#_a(w) - \#_b(w) = 4n,\ n \in \mathbb{Z} \}$$
The automaton therefore tracks the difference between the number of symbols $a$ and $b$ modulo 4.

task.title: Even number of 0s and 1s
task.specification:
Design a deterministic finite automaton, or its state diagram, that recognizes the language $L_A$.
$$L_A = \{ w \in \{0,1\}^* \mid \#_0(w) + \#_1(w) = 2n,\ n \in \mathbb{Z} \}$$
This means that the automaton accepts words of even length over the alphabet $\{0,1\}$.

task.title: Words of the form (ab)*
task.specification:
Design a deterministic finite automaton that recognizes the language defined by the regular expression $(ab)^*$.
$$L = \{ w \in \{a,b\}^* \mid w \text{ matches the regular expression } (ab)^* \}$$
This means that the word consists of any number of repetitions of the pair $ab$. The empty word $\varepsilon$ belongs to the language.
task.verification: (ab)*

task.title: Words of the form a*b*
task.specification:
Design a deterministic finite automaton that recognizes the language defined by the regular expression $a^*b^*$.
$$L = \{ w \in \{a,b\}^* \mid w \text{ matches the regular expression } a^*b^* \}$$
This means that the word consists of any number of symbols $a$ followed by any number of symbols $b$.
task.verification: a*b*

task.title: Words ending with abb
task.specification:
Design a deterministic finite automaton that recognizes all words over the alphabet $\{a,b\}$ ending with the substring $abb$.
$$L = \{ w \in \{a,b\}^* \mid w \text{ ends with } abb \}$$
Examples of words belonging to the language: $abb$, $aabb$, $bababb$.
task.verification: (a|b)*abb

task.title: Words not containing aaa
task.specification:
Design a deterministic finite automaton that recognizes the language of words over the alphabet $\{a,b\}$ that do not contain the substring $aaa$.
$$L = \{ w \in \{a,b\}^* \mid aaa \text{ is not a substring of } w \}$$
The automaton must reject every word containing at least three consecutive symbols $a$.
task.verification: <description /> <created>3. 6. 2026 18:22:21</created> <modified>3. 6. 2026 18:22:21</modified> </meta> <machine type="TM"> <tapes> <tape id="0"> <head id="0" position="0" /> <cell position="0">b</cell> <cell position="1">a</cell> <cell position="2">a</cell> <cell position="3">a</cell> <cell position="4">a</cell> </tape> </tapes> <states> <state id="q1"> <name>q1</name> <comment /> <x>278</x> <y>104</y> <final /> </state> <state id="q0"> <name>q0</name> <comment /> <x>102</x> <y>255</y> <initial /> <final /> </state> <state id="qf"> <name>qf</name> <comment /> <x>770</x> <y>238</y> </state> <state id="q2"> <name>q2</name> <comment /> <x>494</x> <y>212</y> <final /> </state> </states> <transitions> <transition> <from>q0</from> <to>q1</to> <read>a</read> <write>a</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q0</from> <to>q0</to> <read>b</read> <write>b</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q1</from> <to>q0</to> <read>b</read> <write>b</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q1</from> <to>q2</to> <read>a</read> <write>a</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q2</from> <to>q0</to> <read>b</read> <write>b</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q2</from> <to>qf</to> <read>a</read> <write>a</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qf</from> <to>qf</to> <read>a</read> <write>a</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qf</from> <to>qf</to> <read>b</read> <write>b</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> </transitions> <code> δ (q0,a) = (q1) δ (q0,b) = (q0) δ (q1,b) = (q0) δ (q1,a) = (q2) δ (q2,b) = (q0) δ (q2,a) = (qf) δ (qf,a) = (qf) δ (qf,b) = (qf) </code> </machine> </turingmachine>

task.title: Words not containing aa or bbb
task.specification:
Design a deterministic finite automaton that recognizes all words over the alphabet $\{a,b\}$ that do not contain the substrings $aa$ or $bbb$.
$$L = \{ w \in \{a,b\}^* \mid aa \not\subseteq w \land bbb \not\subseteq w \}$$
This means that the word must not contain two consecutive symbols $a$ or three consecutive symbols $b$.
task.verification: <description /> <created>3. 6. 2026 18:32:11</created> <modified>3. 6. 2026 18:32:11</modified> </meta> <machine type="TM"> <tapes> <tape id="0"> <head id="0" position="0" /> <cell position="0">b</cell> <cell position="1">b</cell> <cell position="2">a</cell> <cell position="3">a</cell> </tape> </tapes> <states> <state id="q1"> <name>q1</name> <comment /> <x>275</x> <y>82</y> <final /> </state> <state id="q0"> <name>q0</name> <comment /> <x>57</x> <y>321</y> <initial /> <final /> </state> <state id="qf"> <name>qf</name> <comment /> <x>621</x> <y>124</y> </state> <state id="q2"> <name>q2</name> <comment /> <x>368</x> <y>335</y> <final /> </state> <state id="q3"> <name>q3</name> <comment /> <x>612</x> <y>250</y> <final /> </state> </states> <transitions> <transition> <from>q0</from> <to>q1</to> <read>a</read> <write>a</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q1</from> <to>qf</to> <read>a</read> <write>a</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qf</from> <to>qf</to> <read>a</read> <write>a</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qf</from> <to>qf</to> <read>b</read> <write>b</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q0</from> <to>q2</to> <read>b</read> <write>b</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q2</from> <to>q3</to> <read>b</read> <write>b</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q3</from> <to>qf</to> <read>b</read> <write>b</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q1</from> <to>q2</to> <read>b</read> <write>b</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q2</from> <to>q1</to> <read>a</read> <write>a</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q3</from> <to>q1</to> <read>a</read> <write>a</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> </transitions> <code> δ (q0,a) = (q1) δ (q1,a) = (qf) δ (qf,a) = (qf) δ (qf,b) = (qf) δ (q0,b) = (q2) δ (q2,b) = (q3) δ (q3,b) = (qf) δ (q1,b) = (q2) δ (q2,a) = (q1) δ (q3,a) = (q1) </code> </machine> </turingmachine>

task.title: Substring abba
task.specification:
Design a deterministic finite automaton that recognizes all words over the alphabet $\{a,b\}$ that contain at least one occurrence of the substring $abba$.
$$L = \{ w \in \{a,b\}^* \mid w \text{ contains the substring } abba \}$$
This means that the substring $abba$ appears in the word at least once.
task.verification: <description /> <created>26. 4. 2026 12:21:03</created> <modified>26. 4. 2026 12:21:03</modified> </meta> <machine type="TM"> <tapes> <tape id="0"> <head id="0" position="0" /> </tape> </tapes> <states> <state id="q1"> <name>q1</name> <comment /> <x>350</x> <y>101</y> </state> <state id="q0"> <name>q0</name> <comment /> <x>150</x> <y>220</y> <initial /> </state> <state id="qf"> <name>qf</name> <comment /> <x>928</x> <y>221</y> <final /> </state> <state id="q2"> <name>q2</name> <comment /> <x>626</x> <y>96</y> </state> <state id="q3"> <name>q3</name> <comment /> <x>779</x> <y>212</y> </state> </states> <transitions> <transition> <from>q0</from> <to>q1</to> <read>a</read> <write>a</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q0</from> <to>q0</to> <read>b</read> <write>b</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q1</from> <to>q2</to> <read>b</read> <write>b</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q1</from> <to>q1</to> <read>a</read> <write>a</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q2</from> <to>q3</to> <read>b</read> <write>b</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q2</from> <to>q1</to> <read>a</read> <write>a</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q3</from> <to>qf</to> <read>a</read> <write>a</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q3</from> <to>q0</to> <read>b</read> <write>b</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qf</from> <to>qf</to> <read>a</read> <write>a</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qf</from> <to>qf</to> <read>b</read> <write>b</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> </transitions> <code> δ (q0,a) = (q1) δ (q0,b) = (q0) δ (q1,b) = (q2) δ (q1,a) = (q1) δ (q2,b) = (q3) δ (q2,a) = (q1) δ (q3,a) = (qf) δ (q3,b) = (q0) δ (qf,a) = (qf) δ (qf,b) = (qf) </code> </machine> </turingmachine>

task.title: Every a is followed by b
task.specification:
Design a deterministic finite automaton that recognizes all words over the alphabet $\{a,b\}$ in which every symbol $a$ is immediately followed by the symbol $b$.
$$L = \{ w \in \{a,b\}^* \mid \forall a \text{ in } w \text{, the following symbol is } b \}$$
No symbol $a$ may appear at the end of the word or immediately before another symbol $a$.
task.verification: (b|ab)*

task.title: Words of the form a^n b^n
task.specification:
Design a pushdown automaton that recognizes the language of words of the form $a^n b^n$.
$$L = \{ a^n b^n \mid n \geq 0 \}$$
This means that the number of symbols $a$ must be equal to the number of symbols $b$, and all symbols $a$ must appear before the symbols $b$.
task.verification: 1. Words of the form a^n b^n Recognition of words of the form a^n b^n, where n ≥ 0 8. 5. 2026 21:16:22 8. 5. 2026 21:16:22 0 55 191 1 181 202 2 319 204 3 436 199 1 1 a a Right 2 3 Blank Blank Right 2 2 b b Right 1 2 b b Right 0 1 a a Right δ (1,a,A) = (1,AA) δ (2,Blank,Z) = (3,Z) δ (2,b,A) = (2,) δ (1,b,A) = (2,) δ (0,a,Z) = (1,AZ)

task.title: Words of the form a^n b^n c*
task.specification:
Design a pushdown automaton that recognizes the language of words of the form $a^n b^n c^k$.
$$L = \{ a^n b^n c^k \mid n,k \geq 0 \}$$
The number of symbols $a$ and $b$ must be equal. They may be followed by any number of symbols $c$.
task.verification: <description /> <created>3. 6. 2026 19:03:42</created> <modified>3. 6. 2026 19:03:42</modified> </meta> <machine type="TM"> <tapes> <tape id="0"> <head id="0" position="0" /> <name>q2</name> <comment /> <x>340</x> <y>40</y> </state> <state id="qf"> <name>qf</name> <comment /> <x>477</x> <y>98</y> <final /> </state> </states> <transitions> <transition> <from>q0</from> <to>q0</to> <read>a</read> <write>a</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q0</from> <to>q0</to> <read>a</read> <write>a</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q0</from> <to>q1</to> <read>b</read> <write>b</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q1</from> <to>q1</to> <read>b</read> <write>b</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q0</from> <to>q2</to> <read>c</read> <write>c</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q1</from> <to>q2</to> <read>c</read> <write>c</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q2</from> <to>q2</to> <read>c</read> <write>c</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q0</from> <to>qf</to> <read>Blank</read> <write>Blank</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q1</from> <to>qf</to> <read>Blank</read> <write>Blank</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q2</from> <to>qf</to> <read>Blank</read> <write>Blank</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> </transitions> <code>δ(q0,a,Z) = (q0,AZ) δ(q0,a,A) = (q0,AA) δ(q0,b,A) = (q1,) δ(q1,b,A) = (q1,) δ(q0,c,Z) = (q2,Z) δ(q1,c,Z) = (q2,Z) δ(q2,c,Z) = (q2,Z) δ(q0,Blank,Z) = (qf,Z) δ(q1,Blank,Z) = (qf,Z) δ(q2,Blank,Z) = (qf,Z)</code> </machine> </turingmachine>

task.title: Balanced parentheses ( )
task.specification:
Design a pushdown automaton that recognizes the language of correctly balanced parenthesized expressions.
$$L = \{ w \in \{(,)\}^* \mid w \text{ is correctly balanced} \}$$
Every opening parenthesis must have a corresponding closing parenthesis.
task.verification: <description /> <created>3. 6. 2026 19:18:25</created> <modified>3. 6. 2026 19:18:25</modified> </meta> <machine type="TM"> <tapes> <tape id="0"> <head id="0" position="0" /> <cell position="0">(</cell> <cell position="1">(</cell> <cell position="2">(</cell> <cell position="3">)</cell> <cell position="4">(</cell> <cell position="5">(</cell> <cell position="6">)</cell> <cell position="7">)</cell> </tape> </tapes> <states> <state id="q0"> <name>q0</name> <comment /> <x>40</x> <y>40</y> <initial /> </state> <state id="qf"> <name>qf</name> <comment /> <x>190</x> <y>40</y> <final /> </state> </states> <transitions> <transition> <from>q0</from> <to>q0</to> <read>(</read> <write>(</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q0</from> <to>q0</to> <read>(</read> <write>(</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q0</from> <to>q0</to> <read>)</read> <write>)</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q0</from> <to>qf</to> <read>Blank</read> <write>Blank</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> </transitions> <code>δ(q0,(,Z) = (q0,AZ) δ(q0,(,A) = (q0,AA) δ(q0,),A) = (q0,) δ(q0,Blank,Z) = (qf,Z)</code> </machine> </turingmachine>

task.title: Balanced parentheses ( ) and [ ]
task.specification:
Design a pushdown automaton that recognizes the language of correctly balanced expressions over the alphabet containing the brackets () and [].
$$ L = \{ w \in \{(,),[,]\}^* \mid w \text{ is correctly balanced} \} $$
Every opening bracket must have a corresponding closing bracket of the same type, and the brackets must be correctly nested.
Examples of words belonging to the language:
$$ \varepsilon,\ (),\ [],\ ([]),\ [()],\ (()[]) $$
task.verification: <description /> <created>3. 6. 2026 19:25:26</created> <modified>3. 6. 2026 19:25:26</modified> </meta> <machine type="TM"> <tapes> <tape id="0"> <head id="0" position="0" /> <cell position="0">(</cell> <cell position="1">(</cell> <cell position="2">[</cell> <cell position="3">]</cell> <cell position="4">[</cell> <cell position="5">[</cell> <cell position="6">]</cell> <cell position="14">)</cell> <cell position="15">)</cell> </tape> </tapes> <states> <state id="q0"> <name>q0</name> <comment /> <x>40</x> <y>40</y> <initial /> </state> <state id="qf"> <name>qf</name> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q0</from> <to>q0</to> <read>[</read> <write>[</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q0</from> <to>q0</to> <read>[</read> <write>[</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q0</from> <to>q0</to> <read>[</read> <write>[</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q0</from> <to>q0</to> <read>)</read> <write>)</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q0</from> <to>q0</to> <read>]</read> <write>]</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q0</from> <to>qf</to> <read>Blank</read> <write>Blank</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> </transitions> <code>δ(q0,(,Z) = (q0,AZ) δ(q0,(,A) = (q0,AA) δ(q0,(,B) = (q0,AB) δ(q0,[,Z) = (q0,BZ) δ(q0,[,A) = (q0,BA) δ(q0,[,B) = (q0,BB) δ(q0,),A) = (q0,) δ(q0,],B) = (q0,) δ(q0,Blank,Z) = (qf,Z)</code> </machine> </turingmachine>

task.title: Palindromes over {a,b}*
task.specification:
Design a pushdown automaton that recognizes the language of all even-length palindromes over the alphabet $\{a,b\}$.
$$ L = \{ ww^R \mid w \in \{a,b\}^* \} $$
This means that the second half of the word is the reverse of the first half.
Examples of words belonging to the language:
$$ \varepsilon,\ aa,\ bb,\ abba,\ baab,\ aabbaa $$
task.verification: <tests><test word="" expected="accept" /><test word="aa" expected="accept" /><test word="abba" expected="accept" /><test word="bb" expected="accept" /><test word="baab" expected="accept" /><test word="aabbaa" expected="accept" /><test word="abaaba" expected="accept" /><test word="abbaabba" expected="accept" /><test word="aaabbbbaaa" expected="accept" /><test word="baaabbbbaaab" expected="accept" /><test word="a" expected="reject" /><test word="b" expected="reject" /><test word="ab" expected="reject" /><test word="ba" expected="reject" /><test word="bba" expected="reject" /><test word="aab" expected="reject" /><test word="aba" expected="reject" /><test word="bab" expected="reject" /><test word="bbaa" expected="reject" /><test word="aabb" expected="reject" /><test word="aabab" expected="reject" /><test word="aaba" expected="reject" /><test word="bbab" expected="reject" /><test word="bbabb" expected="reject" /><test word="bbba" expected="reject" /></tests>

task.title: Words of the form a^n b^{2n}
task.specification:
Design a Turing machine that recognizes the language of words of the form $a^n b^{2n}$.
$$L = \{ a^n b^{2n} \mid n \geq 0 \}$$
This means that for each symbol $a$ there must be exactly two symbols $b$.
task.verification: <description /> <created>3. 6. 2026 19:52:25</created> <modified>3. 6. 2026 19:52:25</modified> </meta> <machine type="TM"> <tapes> <tape id="0"> <head id="0" position="-4" /> <cell <write>a_</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q1</from> <to>q1</to> <read>a</read> <write>a</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q1</from> <to>q1</to> <read>b_</read> <write>b_</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q1</from> <to>q2</to> <read>b</read> <write>b_</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q2</from> <to>q2</to> <read>b_</read> <write>b_</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q2</from> <to>q3</to> <read>b</read> <write>b_</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q3</from> <to>q3</to> <read>b_</read> <write>b_</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q3</from> <to>q3</to> <read>b</read> <write>b</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q3</from> <to>q3</to> <read>a</read> <write>a</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q3</from> <to>q0</to> <read>a_</read> <write>a_</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q0</from> <to>q4</to> <read>b_</read> <write>b_</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q4</from> <to>q4</to> <read>b_</read> <write>b_</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q4</from> <to>qf</to> <read>Blank</read> <write>Blank</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q0</from> <to>qf</to> <read>Blank</read> <write>Blank</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> </transitions> <code>// Mark the first unmarked a δ(q0,a) = (q1,a_,R) // Skip all unmarked a symbols δ(q1,a) = (q1,a,R) // Find the first unmarked b and mark it δ(q1,b_) = (q1,b_,R) δ(q1,b) = (q2,b_,R) // Find the second unmarked b and mark it δ(q2,b_) = (q2,b_,R) δ(q2,b) = (q3,b_,L) // Return left to the marked a δ(q3,b_) = (q3,b_,L) δ(q3,b) = (q3,b,L) δ(q3,a) = (q3,a,L) δ(q3,a_) = (q0,a_,R) // If there is no unmarked a left, check that only marked b symbols remain δ(q0,b_) = (q4,b_,R) δ(q4,b_) = (q4,b_,R) // If the end of the tape is reached, accept δ(q4,Blank) = (qf,Blank,R) // Empty word δ(q0,Blank) = (qf,Blank,R)</code> </machine> </turingmachine>

task.title: Unary number multiplication
task.specification:
Design a Turing machine that performs multiplication of two unary numbers.
$$ L = \{ 1^m * 1^n = 1^{m \cdot n} \mid m,n \geq 0 \} $$
The input is written in the form $1^m * 1^n =$.
The Turing machine must write the multiplication result in unary form after the symbol $=$.
Examples:
$ 1*1= \rightarrow 1*1=1 $
$ 11*111= \rightarrow 11*111=111111 $
$ 111*11= \rightarrow 111*11=111111 $
task.verification: <description /> <created>3. 6. 2026 20:06:51</created> <modified>3. 6. 2026 20:06:51</modified> </meta> <machine type="TM"> <tapes> <tape id="0"> <head id="0" position="-13" /> <cell position="-13">1</cell> <cell position="-12">1</cell> <cell position="-11">1</cell> <cell position="-10">1</cell> <cell position="-9">*</cell> <cell position="-8">1</cell> <cell position="-7">1</cell> <cell position="-6">1</cell> <cell position="-5">=</cell> </tape> </tapes> <states> <state id="q1"> <name>q1</name> <comment /> <x>40</x> <y>40</y> </state> <state id="q0"> <name>q0</name> <comment /> <x>190</x> <y>40</y> <initial /> </state> <state id="qClean"> <name>qClean</name> <comment /> <x>340</x> <y>40</y> </state> <state id="q2"> <name>q2</name> <comment /> <x>490</x> <y>40</y> </state> <state id="q3"> <name>q3</name> <comment /> <x>640</x> <y>40</y> </state> <state id="q5"> <name>q5</name> <comment /> <x>40</x> <y>190</y> </state> <state id="q4"> <name>q4</name> <comment /> <x>190</x> <y>190</y> </state> <state id="qBack"> <name>qBack</name> <comment /> <x>340</x> <y>190</y> </state> <state id="q6"> <name>q6</name> <comment /> <x>490</x> <y>190</y> </state> <state id="qf"> <name>qf</name> <comment /> <x>640</x> <y>190</y> <final /> </state> </states> <transitions> <transition> <from>q0</from> <to>q1</to> <read>1</read> <write>X</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q0</from> <to>q0</to> <read>X</read> <write>X</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q0</from> <to>qClean</to> <read>*</read> <write>*</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q1</from> <to>q1</to> <read>1</read> <write>1</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q1</from> <to>q1</to> <read>X</read> <write>X</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q1</from> <to>q2</to> <read>*</read> <write>*</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q2</from> <to>q2</to> <read>Y</read> <write>Y</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q2</from> <to>q3</to> <read>1</read> <write>Y</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q2</from> <to>q5</to> <read>=</read> <write>=</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q3</from> <to>q3</to> <read>1</read> <write>1</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q3</from> <to>q3</to> <read>Y</read> <write>Y</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q3</from> <to>q4</to> <read>=</read> <write>=</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q4</from> <to>q4</to> <read>1</read> <write>1</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q4</from> <to>qBack</to> <read>Blank</read> <write>1</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qBack</from> <to>qBack</to> <read>1</read> <write>1</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qBack</from> <to>qBack</to> <read>Y</read> <write>Y</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qBack</from> <to>qBack</to> <read>=</read> <write>=</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qBack</from> <to>q2</to> <read>*</read> <write>*</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q5</from> <to>q5</to> <read>Y</read> <write>1</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q5</from> <to>q5</to> <read>1</read> <write>1</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q5</from> <to>q6</to> <read>*</read> <write>*</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q6</from> <to>q6</to> <read>X</read> <write>X</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q6</from> <to>q6</to> <read>1</read> <write>1</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q6</from> <to>q0</to> <read>Blank</read> <write>Blank</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qClean</from> <to>qClean</to> <read>X</read> <write>1</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qClean</from> <to>qClean</to> <read>1</read> <write>1</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qClean</from> <to>qf</to> <read>Blank</read> <write>Blank</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> </transitions> <code>// Find the next unprocessed unary symbol of the left number δ(q0,1) = (q1,X,R) δ(q0,X) = (q0,X,R) δ(q0,*) = (qClean,*,L) // Move to the right number δ(q1,1) = (q1,1,R) δ(q1,X) = (q1,X,R) δ(q1,*) = (q2,*,R) // Find a unary symbol in the right number δ(q2,Y) = (q2,Y,R) δ(q2,1) = (q3,Y,R) δ(q2,=) = (q5,=,L) // Move past the = symbol δ(q3,1) = (q3,1,R) δ(q3,Y) = (q3,Y,R) δ(q3,=) = (q4,=,R) // Find the end of the result and append 1 δ(q4,1) = (q4,1,R) δ(q4,Blank) = (qBack,1,L) // Return to the beginning of the right number δ(qBack,1) = (qBack,1,L) δ(qBack,Y) = (qBack,Y,L) δ(qBack,=) = (qBack,=,L) δ(qBack,*) = (q2,*,R) // Restore the right number: Y -> 1 δ(q5,Y) = (q5,1,L) δ(q5,1) = (q5,1,L) δ(q5,*) = (q6,*,L) // Return to the beginning of the left number δ(q6,X) = (q6,X,L) δ(q6,1) = (q6,1,L) δ(q6,Blank) = (q0,Blank,R) // Cleanup: X -> 1 δ(qClean,X) = (qClean,1,L) δ(qClean,1) = (qClean,1,L) δ(qClean,Blank) = (qf,Blank,R)</code> </machine> </turingmachine>

task.title: Unary number subtraction
task.specification:
Design a Turing machine that performs subtraction of two unary numbers.
$$ L = \{ 1^m - 1^n = 1^{m-n} \mid m,n \geq 0 \land m \geq n \} $$
The input is written in the form $1^m - 1^n =$. The Turing machine must write the subtraction result in unary form after the symbol $=$.
Examples:
$ 111-1= \rightarrow 111-1=11 $
$ 11111-11= \rightarrow 11111-11=111 $
$ 111-111= \rightarrow 111-111= $
We assume that the first number is greater than or equal to the second number.
task.verification: <description /> <created>3. 6. 2026 20:27:44</created> <modified>3. 6. 2026 20:27:44</modified> </meta> <machine type="TM"> <tapes> <tape id="0"> <head id="0" position="-6" /> <cell position="-6">1</cell> <cell position="-5">1</cell> <cell position="-4">1</cell> <cell position="-3">1</cell> <cell position="-2">1</cell> <cell position="-1">-</cell> <cell position="0">1</cell> <cell position="1">1</cell> <cell position="2">=</cell> </tape> </tapes> <states> <state id="q0"> <name>q0</name> <comment /> <x>40</x> <y>40</y> <initial /> </state> <state id="q1"> <name>q1</name> <comment /> <x>190</x> <y>40</y> </state> <state id="q2"> <name>q2</name> <comment /> <x>340</x> <y>40</y> </state> <state id="qCopy"> <name>qCopy</name> <comment /> <x>490</x> <y>40</y> </state> <state id="q3"> <name>q3</name> <comment /> <x>640</x> <y>40</y> </state> <state id="q4"> <name>q4</name> <comment /> <x>40</x> <y>190</y> </state> <state id="qFindLeft"> <name>qFindLeft</name> <comment /> <x>301</x> <y>360</y> </state> <state id="qMarkCopy"> <name>qMarkCopy</name> <comment /> <x>340</x> <y>190</y> </state> <state id="qClean"> <name>qClean</name> <comment /> <x>499</x> <y>346</y> </state> <state id="qWriteResult"> <name>qWriteResult</name> <comment /> <x>640</x> <y>190</y> </state> <state id="qReturnLeft"> <name>qReturnLeft</name> <comment /> <x>40</x> <y>340</y> </state> <state id="qf"> <name>qf</name> <comment /> <x>684</x> <y>329</y> <final /> </state> </states> <transitions> <transition> <from>q0</from> <to>q0</to> <read>1</read> <write>1</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q0</from> <to>q1</to> <read>-</read> <write>-</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q1</from> <to>q1</to> <read>Y</read> <write>Y</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q1</from> <to>q2</to> <read>1</read> <write>Y</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q1</from> <to>qCopy</to> <read>=</read> <write>=</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q2</from> <to>q2</to> <read>Y</read> <write>Y</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q2</from> <to>q2</to> <read>1</read> <write>1</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q2</from> <to>q3</to> <read>-</read> <write>-</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q3</from> <to>q3</to> <read>X</read> <write>X</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q3</from> <to>q4</to> <read>1</read> <write>X</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q4</from> <to>q4</to> <read>X</read> <write>X</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q4</from> <to>q4</to> <read>1</read> <write>1</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q4</from> <to>q1</to> <read>-</read> <write>-</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qCopy</from> <to>qCopy</to> <read>Y</read> <write>Y</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qCopy</from> <to>qCopy</to> <read>1</read> <write>1</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qCopy</from> <to>qFindLeft</to> <read>-</read> <write>-</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qFindLeft</from> <to>qFindLeft</to> <read>X</read> <write>X</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qFindLeft</from> <to>qMarkCopy</to> <read>1</read> <write>X</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qFindLeft</from> <to>qClean</to> <read>Blank</read> <write>Blank</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qMarkCopy</from> <to>qMarkCopy</to> <read>X</read> <write>X</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qMarkCopy</from> <to>qMarkCopy</to> <read>1</read> <write>1</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qMarkCopy</from> <to>qMarkCopy</to> <read>-</read> <write>-</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qMarkCopy</from> <to>qMarkCopy</to> <read>Y</read> <write>Y</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qMarkCopy</from> <to>qWriteResult</to> <read>=</read> <write>=</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qWriteResult</from> <to>qWriteResult</to> <read>1</read> <write>1</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qWriteResult</from> <to>qReturnLeft</to> <read>Blank</read> <write>1</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qReturnLeft</from> <to>qReturnLeft</to> <read>1</read> <write>1</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qReturnLeft</from> <to>qReturnLeft</to> <read>=</read> <write>=</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qReturnLeft</from> <to>qReturnLeft</to> <read>Y</read> <write>Y</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qReturnLeft</from> <to>qFindLeft</to> <read>-</read> <write>-</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qClean</from> <to>qClean</to> <read>X</read> <write>1</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qClean</from> <to>qClean</to> <read>1</read> <write>1</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qClean</from> <to>qClean</to> <read>-</read> <write>-</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qClean</from> <to>qClean</to> <read>Y</read> <write>1</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qClean</from> <to>qf</to> <read>=</read> <write>=</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> </transitions> <code>// Find a unary symbol in the second number δ(q0,1) = (q0,1,R) δ(q0,-) = (q1,-,R) δ(q1,Y) = (q1,Y,R) δ(q1,1) = (q2,Y,L) δ(q1,=) = (qCopy,=,L) // Return left to the first number δ(q2,Y) = (q2,Y,L) δ(q2,1) = (q2,1,L) δ(q2,-) = (q3,-,L) // Find an unmarked unary symbol in the first number and mark it δ(q3,X) = (q3,X,L) δ(q3,1) = (q4,X,R) // Return to the second number δ(q4,X) = (q4,X,R) δ(q4,1) = (q4,1,R) δ(q4,-) = (q1,-,R) // Copy the remaining unary symbols of the first number after = δ(qCopy,Y) = (qCopy,Y,L) δ(qCopy,1) = (qCopy,1,L) δ(qCopy,-) = (qFindLeft,-,L) // Find the remaining unmarked 1 in the first number δ(qFindLeft,X) = (qFindLeft,X,L) δ(qFindLeft,1) = (qMarkCopy,X,R) δ(qFindLeft,Blank) = (qClean,Blank,R) // Move past = and append a result 1 δ(qMarkCopy,X) = (qMarkCopy,X,R) δ(qMarkCopy,1) = (qMarkCopy,1,R) δ(qMarkCopy,-) = (qMarkCopy,-,R) δ(qMarkCopy,Y) = (qMarkCopy,Y,R) δ(qMarkCopy,=) = (qWriteResult,=,R) δ(qWriteResult,1) = (qWriteResult,1,R) δ(qWriteResult,Blank) = (qReturnLeft,1,L) // Return to the beginning for further copying δ(qReturnLeft,1) = (qReturnLeft,1,L) δ(qReturnLeft,=) = (qReturnLeft,=,L) δ(qReturnLeft,Y) = (qReturnLeft,Y,L) δ(qReturnLeft,-) = (qFindLeft,-,L) // Cleanup: X a Y restore na 1 δ(qClean,X) = (qClean,1,R) δ(qClean,1) = (qClean,1,R) δ(qClean,-) = (qClean,-,R) δ(qClean,Y) = (qClean,1,R) δ(qClean,=) = (qf,=,R)</code> </machine> </turingmachine>

task.title: Words of the form a^n b^n c^n
task.specification:
Design a Turing machine that recognizes the language of words of the form $a^n b^n c^n$.
$$ L = \{ a^n b^n c^n \mid n \geq 0 \} $$
This means that the word consists of the same number of symbols $a$, $b$ and $c$, where all symbols $a$ come first, followed by all symbols $b$, and finally all symbols $c$.
Examples of words belonging to the language:
$$ \varepsilon,\ abc,\ aabbcc,\ aaabbbccc $$
Examples of words not belonging to the language:
$$ ab,\ abbcc,\ aabcc,\ abcc,\ acb $$
task.verification: <description /> <created>3. 6. 2026 20:34:28</created> <modified>3. 6. 2026 20:34:28</modified> </meta> <machine type="TM"> <tapes> <tape id="0"> <head id="0" position="-8" /> <cell position="-8">a</cell> <cell position="-7">a</cell> <cell position="-6">a</cell> <cell position="-5">b</cell> <cell position="-4">b</cell> <cell position="-3">c</cell> <cell position="-2">c</cell> <cell position="-1">c</cell> </tape> </tapes> <states> <state id="q1"> <name>q1</name> <comment /> <x>40</x> <y>40</y> </state> <state id="q0"> <name>q0</name> <comment /> <x>190</x> <y>40</y> <initial /> </state> <state id="qCheck"> <name>qCheck</name> <comment /> <x>340</x> <y>40</y> </state> <state id="qf"> <name>qf</name> <comment /> <x>490</x> <y>40</y> <final /> </state> <state id="q2"> <name>q2</name> <comment /> <x>640</x> <y>40</y> </state> <state id="q3"> <name>q3</name> <comment /> <x>40</x> <y>190</y> </state> </states> <transitions> <transition> <from>q0</from> <to>q1</to> <read>a</read> <write>a_</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q0</from> <to>q0</to> <read>a_</read> <write>a_</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q0</from> <to>qCheck</to> <read>b_</read> <write>b_</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q0</from> <to>qCheck</to> <read>c_</read> <write>c_</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q0</from> <to>qf</to> <read>Blank</read> <write>Blank</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q1</from> <to>q1</to> <read>a</read> <write>a</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q1</from> <to>q1</to> <read>a_</read> <write>a_</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q1</from> <to>q1</to> <read>b_</read> <write>b_</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q1</from> <to>q2</to> <read>b</read> <write>b_</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q2</from> <to>q2</to> <read>b</read> <write>b</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q2</from> <to>q2</to> <read>b_</read> <write>b_</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q2</from> <to>q2</to> <read>c_</read> <write>c_</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q2</from> <to>q3</to> <read>c</read> <write>c_</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q3</from> <to>q3</to> <read>c</read> <write>c</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q3</from> <to>q3</to> <read>c_</read> <write>c_</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q3</from> <to>q3</to> <read>b</read> <write>b</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q3</from> <to>q3</to> <read>b_</read> <write>b_</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q3</from> <to>q3</to> <read>a</read> <write>a</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q3</from> <to>q3</to> <read>a_</read> <write>a_</write> <move>Left</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>q3</from> <to>q0</to> <read>Blank</read> <write>Blank</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qCheck</from> <to>qCheck</to> <read>b_</read> <write>b_</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qCheck</from> <to>qCheck</to> <read>c_</read> <write>c_</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> <transition> <from>qCheck</from> <to>qf</to> <read>Blank</read> <write>Blank</write> <move>Right</move> <comment /> </transition> </transitions> <code>// Nájdeme prvé neoznačené a δ(q0,a) = (q1,a_,R) δ(q0,a_) = (q0,a_,R) // Ak už nie je žiadne a, skontrolujeme zvyšok δ(q0,b_) = (qCheck,b_,R) δ(q0,c_) = (qCheck,c_,R) δ(q0,Blank) = (qf,Blank,R) // Nájdeme prvé neoznačené b δ(q1,a) = (q1,a,R) δ(q1,a_) = (q1,a_,R) δ(q1,b_) = (q1,b_,R) δ(q1,b) = (q2,b_,R) // Nájdeme prvé neoznačené c δ(q2,b) = (q2,b,R) δ(q2,b_) = (q2,b_,R) δ(q2,c_) = (q2,c_,R) δ(q2,c) = (q3,c_,L) // Vrátime sa na začiatok δ(q3,c) = (q3,c,L) δ(q3,c_) = (q3,c_,L) δ(q3,b) = (q3,b,L) δ(q3,b_) = (q3,b_,L) δ(q3,a) = (q3,a,L) δ(q3,a_) = (q3,a_,L) δ(q3,Blank) = (q0,Blank,R) // Kontrola, či už ostali iba označené b a c δ(qCheck,b_) = (qCheck,b_,R) δ(qCheck,c_) = (qCheck,c_,R) δ(qCheck,Blank) = (qf,Blank,R)</code> </machine> </turingmachine>
